skill.name: social-media-finder-skill
skill.description: This skill helps users automatically find social media profiles across platforms like Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, LinkedIn, etc. using the BrowserAct API. Agent should proactively apply this skill when users express needs like finding someone's social media accounts, discovering a brand's social media presence, tracking down social profiles of job candidates, finding contact info for sales prospects, researching a person's digital footprint, verifying the identity of someone met online, monitoring the social media reach of influencers, checking social accounts of business competitors, gathering public profiles for background research, locating official customer service accounts across platforms, or compiling contact databases for networking.
skill.summary: Automatically discover social media profiles across multiple platforms and retrieve profile URLs, follower counts, and bios in CSV format.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 50 potential issues, but after semantic evaluation, all findings are false positives. The skill makes network calls to the legitimate BrowserAct API service using standard Bearer token authentication. The external command references in SKILL.md are documentation examples, not executable code. Template ID strings were incorrectly flagged as weak crypto. No malicious behavior detected - this is a legitimate third-party API integration skill.

low_findings.title: Documentation References to Shell Commands
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains backtick-formatted command examples. These are documentation examples, not executable code. The actual script invocation uses a safe Python subprocess pattern with hardcoded arguments.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backtick syntax in markdown is used for inline code formatting. The documented commands are examples showing how to invoke the skill, not actual shell execution vulnerabilities.

low_findings.title: Network Calls to Third-Party API
low_findings.description: The script makes HTTP requests to api.browseract.com. This is the intended functionality for the skill, not exfiltration. API key is used only for Bearer token authentication with the service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct evidence shows network calls only target the legitimate BrowserAct API endpoint. No data exfiltration to third-party domains detected. Bearer token is used correctly for authentication.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Key
low_findings.description: Accesses BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment. This is standard credential handling for API authentication, not secret exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key is read from environment and used only for Bearer token authentication with BrowserAct. No evidence of key being transmitted to external parties or logged inappropriately.

content.user_title: Find Social Media Profiles Across Platforms
content.value_statement: Manually searching for social media accounts across multiple platforms is time-consuming. This skill automates the discovery process by querying the BrowserAct API, returning profile URLs, follower counts, and bios in a downloadable CSV.
actual_capabilities: Search for social media profiles by person or brand name across Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok, and more
Retrieve profile metadata including follower counts, bios, and direct profile URLs
Export results as CSV files with structured data columns
Handle API authentication and polling for task completion
Monitor execution status with timestamped log output
Handle errors gracefully including invalid credentials and concurrent task limits
limitations: Requires a BrowserAct API key for authentication
Results depend on BrowserAct workflow accuracy and platform availability
Rate limits may apply based on BrowserAct subscription plan
Cannot guarantee finding profiles for all individuals or brands

use_cases.title: Recruiting and Hiring
use_cases.description: Recruiters can research job candidates by finding their social media presence across multiple platforms, helping verify identities and assess professional profiles.

use_cases.title: Sales Prospecting
use_cases.description: Sales teams can gather social media contact information and profile data for prospective clients, enabling more personalized outreach.

use_cases.title: Competitive Research
use_cases.description: Marketing analysts can track competitor brands across social platforms, monitoring follower growth and engagement strategies.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Person's Social Profiles
prompt_templates.prompt: Find social media profiles for [Person Name] using the social-media-finder skill.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to locate someone's social media presence

prompt_templates.title: Research a Brand's Social Presence
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for [Brand Name] across social media platforms and compile their profile URLs.
prompt_templates.scenario: When researching brand visibility and social reach

prompt_templates.title: Build Contact Database
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the social-media-finder skill to gather social media profiles for the following contacts: [List of names]. Save results to CSV.

prompt_templates.title: Verify Online Identity
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all social media accounts associated with [Name] to verify their online presence.
prompt_templates.scenario: When confirming identity for due diligence

output_examples.input: Find social media profiles for Elon Musk
output_examples.output:
Search complete. Found profiles across 4 platforms:

LinkedIn: Profile URL, 75M+ followers
Twitter/X: Profile URL, 170M+ followers
Instagram: Profile URL, 50M+ followers
TikTok: Profile URL, 25M+ followers

CSV file with detailed data available for download.

Brand presence detected across 6 platforms:

Twitter: Profile URL, 20M+ followers
Instagram: Profile URL, 18M+ followers
LinkedIn: Profile URL, 15M+ followers
YouTube: Profile URL, 10M+ subscribers
Facebook: Profile URL, 8M+ followers
TikTok: Profile URL, 5M+ followers

best_practices: Use plus signs (+) between name components for better search accuracy (e.g., John+Smith instead of John Smith)
Verify API key configuration before running searches to avoid authentication errors
Review error messages carefully - they indicate specific issues like concurrent task limits or invalid credentials
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to find social media for private individuals without legitimate purpose
Do not ignore error messages - they contain actionable guidance for resolution
Do not exceed the concurrent task limit specified by your BrowserAct plan

faq.question: What happens if the BrowserAct API key is invalid?
faq.answer: The script will display an authorization error message. You should verify your API key is correct and has not expired.

faq.question: How long does a social media search take?
faq.answer: The task may take several minutes depending on the number of platforms being searched. The script outputs status updates every 10 seconds.

faq.question: Can I search for multiple people in one request?
faq.answer: Each invocation searches for one person or brand. Run the script multiple times for batch searches.

faq.question: What social media platforms are supported?
faq.answer: The BrowserAct workflow searches across major platforms including Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok, and others.

faq.question: What should I do if I hit the concurrent task limit?
faq.answer: Upgrade your BrowserAct plan or wait for existing tasks to complete before starting new searches.

faq.question: Is the data exported in a usable format?
faq.answer: Yes, results are provided as a CSV file containing platform name, follower counts, profile titles, descriptions, and direct profile URLs.
